section_text_parts: Make sure to use yield one single time per dependency.
tutorial/dependencies/dependencies-with-.section_text: Make sure to use yield one single time per dependency.

tutorial/dependencies/dependencies-with-.title: Technical Details
section_text_parts: Any function that is valid to use with:
- @contextlib.contextmanager or
- @contextlib.asynccontextmanager
would be valid to use as a FastAPI dependency.
In fact, FastAPI uses those two decorators internally.
tutorial/dependencies/dependencies-with-.section_text:
Any function that is valid to use with:

- @contextlib.contextmanager or

- @contextlib.asynccontextmanager

would be valid to use as a FastAPI dependency.

In fact, FastAPI uses those two decorators internally.

section_text_parts: You can use async or regular functions.
FastAPI will do the right thing with each, the same as with normal dependencies.
tutorial/dependencies/dependencies-with-.section_text:
You can use async or regular functions.

FastAPI will do the right thing with each, the same as with normal dependencies.

tutorial/dependencies/dependencies-with-.title: Technical Details
section_text_parts: This works thanks to Python's Context Managers .
FastAPI uses them internally to achieve this.
tutorial/dependencies/dependencies-with-.section_text:
This works thanks to Python's Context Managers .

FastAPI uses them internally to achieve this.

section_text_parts: This is a somewhat advanced technique, and in most of the cases you won't really need it, as you can raise exceptions (including HTTPException ) from inside of the rest of your application code, for example, in the path operation function .
But it's there for you if you need it. 🤓
tutorial/dependencies/dependencies-with-.section_text:
This is a somewhat advanced technique, and in most of the cases you won't really need it, as you can raise exceptions (including HTTPException ) from inside of the rest of your application code, for example, in the path operation function .

But it's there for you if you need it. 🤓

section_text_parts: Only one response will be sent to the client. It might be one of the error responses or it will be the response from the path operation .
After one of those responses is sent, no other response can be sent.
tutorial/dependencies/dependencies-with-.section_text:
Only one response will be sent to the client. It might be one of the error responses or it will be the response from the path operation .

After one of those responses is sent, no other response can be sent.

section_text_parts: If you raise any exception in the code from the path operation function , it will be passed to the dependencies with yield, including HTTPException . In most cases you will want to re-raise that same exception or a new one from the dependency with yield to make sure it's properly handled.
tutorial/dependencies/dependencies-with-.section_text: If you raise any exception in the code from the path operation function , it will be passed to the dependencies with yield, including HTTPException . In most cases you will want to re-raise that same exception or a new one from the dependency with yield to make sure it's properly handled.

section_text_parts: This is, more or less, an "advanced" idea.
If you are just starting with FastAPI you might want to skip it for now.
tutorial/dependencies/dependencies-with-.section_text:
This is, more or less, an "advanced" idea.

If you are just starting with FastAPI you might want to skip it for now.

section_text_parts: Another way to create a context manager is with:
- @contextlib.contextmanager or
- @contextlib.asynccontextmanager
using them to decorate a function with a single yield .
That's what FastAPI uses internally for dependencies with yield .
But you don't have to use the decorators for FastAPI dependencies (and you shouldn't).
FastAPI will do it for you internally.
tutorial/dependencies/dependencies-with-.section_text:
Another way to create a context manager is with:

- @contextlib.contextmanager or

- @contextlib.asynccontextmanager

using them to decorate a function with a single yield .

That's what FastAPI uses internally for dependencies with yield .

But you don't have to use the decorators for FastAPI dependencies (and you shouldn't).

FastAPI will do it for you internally.
